mnemonics.title: Configuration Management Key Activities
mnemonics.content: ICVR - Identification, Control, Verification, Release/Status Accounting - Remember: 'I Can Verify Releases'

mnemonics.title: CI Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Hardware, Software, Documentation, Services, People - Remember: 'HSDSP' or 'Help Serve Devs By Setting Projects'

mnemonics.title: CMDB Benefits
mnemonics.content: FARIA - Faster incident resolution, Accurate change impact, Reduced costs, Improved compliance, Audit support - Remember: 'Fast And Reliable Incident Analysis'

mnemonics.title: CMDB Accuracy Pillars
mnemonics.content: AUTO - Automated discovery, User cooperation, Training, Ongoing maintenance - Remember: 'Always Update Things Ongoing'

acronyms.usage: Any component like servers, applications, databases that need to be managed
Centralized database storing all CI information and relationships
Overall system including tools and processes for configuration management
Framework for designing, delivering, managing IT services
Used in CMDB to update and maintain configuration records

visualTips.concept: CMDB as the Heart of ITSM
visualTips.visualization: Imagine CMDB as the heart of ITSM, pumping configuration data to all processes like Change Management (arteries), Incident Management (veins), and Problem Management (capillaries)
Think of a baseline as a photograph - it captures the exact state at that moment, allowing you to compare future states against this reference point
Visualize CI relationships as a tree: Root = Infrastructure, Trunk = Servers, Branches = Applications, Leaves = Individual settings and configurations

analogies.analogy: Like a detailed map of a city - shows all buildings (CIs), roads (connections), landmarks (relationships), and is constantly updated as the city changes
Like a library catalog system - tracks every book (CI), its location, version, who borrowed it, and ensures everything is in the right place
Like taking a snapshot before editing a document - if something goes wrong, you can always return to the original state
Like a security camera that constantly monitors and records what's happening in a building, automatically updating the floor plan
